page.content: The data presented in the table provides a comprehensive overview of the expression levels of S100A2 in relation to various ocular tissues, specifically the cornea and limbus.

page.content: S100A2, a calcium-binding protein, is known to be overexpressed in all epithelial cells, suggesting its potential role in cellular functions and responses. The analysis focuses on the correlation between S100A2 expression and the efficacy of antidiabetic medications, as indicated by the various odds ratios (OR) and associated P values. Notably, the column labeled "Low expression" highlights the prevalence of S100A2 in the plasma membrane of basal epithelial cells, which decreases progressively toward the upper layers, potentially influencing the survival rates of patients with diabetes. Furthermore, the "5 years OS (%)" metric reveals significant survival outcomes, with an overall survival rate of 84% for patients exhibiting high expression levels of S100A2, compared to 62.6% for those with low expression, underscoring the importance of this protein in therapeutic contexts and patient prognoses.
